events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Sir Brian May was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Sir Brian May! The Queen legend, Red Special maestro, and astrophysicist continues to rock the world.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Sir Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Royalty
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young today! Wishing a very happy birthday to the incomparable Mick Jagger. Still got those moves.

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the very first music video ever aired.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Remember when MTV actually played music videos? It all started on this day in 1981, kicking off with The Buggles. A true game-changer.

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' in 1994
events.blurb: Oasis' seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' was released in the UK on August 29, 1994, featuring singles like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Britpop Debut
angles.copy: Twenty-nine years ago today, Oasis dropped their iconic debut 'Definitely Maybe'. The sound of a generation, instantly legendary.

events.title: Woodstock Festival began in 1969
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, began on August 15, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since the mud, music, and magic of Woodstock began. Three days that changed music history forever.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977; he would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley today, 49 years since we lost the King of Rock and Roll. His music lives on.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Legendary rapper Tupac Shakur died on September 13, 1996, six days after being shot in Las Vegas; he would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur today, 30 years since his untimely passing. A voice that defined a generation, gone too soon.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Groundbreaking guitarist Jimi Hendrix passed away on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27; he would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Legend
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who left us 56 years ago. The guitar world was never the same.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' in 1969
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road', featuring classics like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun', was released in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover walk remains one of the most famous in music history.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' formidable frontwoman, Chrissie Hynde, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Pretenders Power
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the legendary Chrissie Hynde! The voice and force behind The Pretenders, still rocking strong.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis and Beady Eye frontman Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing the swagger and the tunes. As you were.

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' in 1975
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK on October 24, 1975.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: On this day, we celebrate the enduring message of John Lennon's 'Imagine', a song that continues to inspire hope and unity.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod in 2001
events.blurb: Apple introduced its groundbreaking first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music.

angles.name: Music In Your Pocket
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple changed how we listen to music forever with the launch of the original iPod. A true tech game-changer.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th marks James Bond Day, celebrating the premiere of the very first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating over six decades of espionage, iconic theme tunes, and shaken-not-stirred martinis. Who's your favourite Bond?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Visionary musician and songwriter John Lennon was born on October 9, 1940; he would have been 86 this year.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the enduring legacy and musical genius of John Lennon. Imagine.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The legendary British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese was born on October 27, 1939.

angles.name: Comedy Icon
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! A true titan of British comedy.

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' in 1968
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released in the UK on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years since The Beatles delivered their sprawling masterpiece, 'The White Album'. A diverse and influential double LP.
